content.text: Interesting — jobs show Complete already. Let me look at the logs and find the resource files.

input.command: pwd; echo "---"; ls -la; echo "---CASES---"; ls cases/ 2>/dev/null; find / -path /proc -prune -o -name 'skew-job-baseline.yaml' -print 2>/dev/null | head

echo "===BASELINE==="; kubectl logs -n spark-skew -l strategy=baseline --tail=30; echo "===BROADCAST==="; kubectl logs -n spark-skew -l strategy=broadcast --tail=30; echo "===AQE==="; kubectl logs -n spark-skew -l strategy=aqe --tail=30

content.text: All three jobs are already in `Complete` state with sensible results. This looks like the work may already be done. Let me verify there are no hidden failures (restarts, failed pods, events) and inspect the job definitions to confirm there are no lingering config bugs.

input.command: echo "===EVENTS (warnings)==="; kubectl get events -n spark-skew --field-selector type=Warning 2>/dev/null | tail -20; echo "===POD DETAIL==="; kubectl get pods -n spark-skew -o wide; echo "===JOB COMPLETIONS==="; kubectl get jobs -n spark-skew -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,SUCCEEDED:.status.succeeded,FAILED:.status.failed
echo "===MASTER LOG tail==="; kubectl logs -n spark-skew -l app=spark-master --tail=8; echo "===WORKER LOG tail==="; kubectl logs -n spark-skew -l app=spark-worker --tail=8 2>/dev/null
